results.name: tool_call_success
results.label: pass
results.reason: The upload_file tool returned a structured JSON response with status set to 'success' and included expected fields (file_id, upload_url) plus a success message. There are no error indicators, exceptions, timeouts, or empty/invalid outputs, so the tool call succeeded. {'failed_tools': ''}

input.content: {"tool_calls": "[TOOL_CALL] upload_file(file_path=\"/documents/report.pdf\", destination=\"cloud_storage\")\n[TOOL_RESULT] {'status': 'success', 'file_id': 'file_12345', 'upload_url': 'https://storage.example.com/file_12345', 'message': 'File uploaded successfully'}", "tool_definitions": "TOOL_DEFINITIONS:\n- upload_file: Upload a file to cloud storage (inputs: file_path, destination)"}

{"tool_calls": "[TOOL_CALL] upload_file(file_path=\"/documents/report.pdf\", destination=\"cloud_storage\")\n[TOOL_RESULT] {'status': 'success', 'file_id': 'file_12345', 'upload_url': 'https://storage.example.com/file_12345', 'message': 'File uploaded successfully'}", "tool_definitions": "TOOL_DEFINITIONS:\n- upload_file: Upload a file to cloud storage (inputs: file_path, destination)"}

tool_definitions.name: query_database
tool_definitions.description: Execute SQL queries on the database

tool_result.message: Unable to connect to database. Connection timeout after 30 seconds.

content.text: I encountered an error while trying to query the database. The connection timed out after 30 seconds. Please try again later or contact your database administrator.

results.name: tool_call_success
results.label: fail
results.reason: The single tool call to query_database returned a result with status set to 'error' and an explicit DB_CONNECTION_FAILED message indicating a connection timeout. This is a technical failure, so the overall evaluation process fails. {'failed_tools': 'query_database'}

input.content: {"tool_calls": "[TOOL_CALL] query_database(table=\"users\", query=\"SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 25\")\n[TOOL_RESULT] {'status': 'error', 'error_code': 'DB_CONNECTION_FAILED', 'message': 'Unable to connect to database. Connection timeout after 30 seconds.'}", "tool_definitions": "TOOL_DEFINITIONS:\n- query_database: Execute SQL queries on the database (inputs: table, query)"}

{"tool_calls": "[TOOL_CALL] query_database(table=\"users\", query=\"SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 25\")\n[TOOL_RESULT] {'status': 'error', 'error_code': 'DB_CONNECTION_FAILED', 'message': 'Unable to connect to database. Connection timeout after 30 seconds.'}", "tool_definitions": "TOOL_DEFINITIONS:\n- query_database: Execute SQL queries on the database (inputs: table, query)"}

RequestBody.instructions:
We just run Python code for an evaluation sample and captured a Python array of print statements.
Validating the printed content to determine if the evaluation completed successfully:
Respond false if any entries show:
- Error messages or exception text (not including normal status messages)
- Malformed or corrupted data
- Actual timeout errors or connection failures
- Explicit failure messages like "Evaluation run failed"
- Exceptions being raised

Respond with true if:
- The evaluation was created and ran
- Status messages showing progress (like "Waiting for eval run to complete... current status: in_progress") are NORMAL and expected
- The evaluation completed with results (passed or failed evaluation metrics are both valid outcomes)
- Resources were cleaned up (agent deleted, evaluation deleted)

Always respond with `reason` indicating the reason for the response.

content.text: {"correct":false,"reason":"The eval and eval run were created and the run reached status \"completed\" with output items. However, the printed output includes an explicit technical failure message inside the evaluation results (tool_call_success item 2: query_database returned status \"error\" with DB_CONNECTION_FAILED / connection timeout, and the evaluator reason says \"overall evaluation process fails\"). Per instructions, explicit failure messages in the entries require returning false."}

ResponseBody.instructions:
We just run Python code for an evaluation sample and captured a Python array of print statements.
Validating the printed content to determine if the evaluation completed successfully:
Respond false if any entries show:
- Error messages or exception text (not including normal status messages)
- Malformed or corrupted data
- Actual timeout errors or connection failures
- Explicit failure messages like "Evaluation run failed"
- Exceptions being raised

Respond with true if:
- The evaluation was created and ran
- Status messages showing progress (like "Waiting for eval run to complete... current status: in_progress") are NORMAL and expected
- The evaluation completed with results (passed or failed evaluation metrics are both valid outcomes)
- Resources were cleaned up (agent deleted, evaluation deleted)

Always respond with `reason` indicating the reason for the response.
